name: Hebridean Scotch Broth
recipeInstructions:
Note: Neck of mutton may be either whole or in chops (use lamb only if necessary).
Start to cook this dish the day before serving. Boil the neck of mutton in a large covered pan with 2-1/2 pints lightly salted water for 2 hours (or longer if needed). Skim off all scum and excess fat as it rises to the surface.
Take out the meat when tender. Add barley and let soak in the stock overnight.
Next day, bring the stock and barley back to a boil. Dice vegetables except leeks and add to the stock; cook for another 60 minutes. Add leeks, cut into fine rings, about 10 minutes before cooking ends.
If desired, strip meat from bones, cut into small pieces, and return it to the soup before reheating thoroughly.
Serve with parsley sprinkled on top. Optionally serve with boiled potatoes (Golden Wonder variety) prepared as described.
Note: The original text included additional instructions for preparing potatoes, which have been omitted here to focus solely on the broth recipe.
